name: Dark Chocolate Torte with Orange Confit Sauce and Zest
recipeIngredient: 300 g dark chocolate, chopped into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. To make the chocolate mousse: bring the cream to a boil. Squeeze the gelatine leaves to remove excess water and add them to the cream with the chocolate. Remove from the heat and stir constantly until the chocolate and gelatine have dissolved. Set aside to cool.
2. When the chocolate cream is cool, fold in the whipped cream and set aside.
3. To make the chocolate sponge: beat the eggs with the sugar until pale and thick. Carefully fold in the sifted flour and cocoa. Line a 20x30cm baking sheet with baking paper and spread the mixture onto the paper to a thickness of 1cm. Bake in a preheated oven at 200°C for about 10 minutes, then remove and set aside to cool.
4. To make the orange confit sauce: melt the sugar in a saucepan with lemon juice and cook until the mixture forms a golden caramel. Remove from the heat and carefully pour the orange juice onto the caramel and stir to combine. Remove from the heat, stir through the orange zest and set aside to cool.
5. To assemble the torte: spread a 2cm thick layer of mousse onto the chocolate sponge, cover and refrigerate until set.
6. To serve: cut the torte into triangular slices, place on a serving plate and dust with cocoa. Spoon around some orange confit sauce, arrange a triangle of chocolate on top and serve with segments of orange.
